meta.method: Each app hand-labeled against its real current docs/pricing with evidence URLs. Honest corrections to initial expectations kept (e.g. fanbasis/Waterfall/DealCloud DO publish public docs; iPayX has no public API; OpenAI now paid_gated).

apps.name: GitHub
apps.notes: Public REST + GraphQL docs; personal access tokens, OAuth apps, and GitHub App tokens are self-serve; GitHub ships an official MCP server.

apps.name: Notion
apps.notes: Public REST docs; internal integration token (self-serve) or OAuth for public integrations; Notion hosts an official MCP server at mcp.notion.com.

apps.name: Grain
apps.notes: Confirmed the grain.com meeting recorder; public REST docs with self-serve Personal/Workspace Access Tokens plus OAuth2, though some third-party sources suggest full API may need a paid plan; only a community MCP (eadm/grain-mcp-server) exists.

apps.name: Plain
apps.notes: Confirmed the plain.com B2B support tool; GraphQL-only API with a self-serve Bearer API key from workspace settings and public docs; Plain ships an official hosted MCP server at mcp.plain.com.

apps.name: Copper
apps.notes: Confirmed the copper.com CRM (not the metal or Copper.co); public REST docs, token+email API key auth (admin-only, requires a paid Copper account with free trial) plus OAuth2 for partners; only third-party/aggregator MCPs (Apideck, viaSocket) found.

apps.name: Twenty
apps.notes: Confirmed the open-source twenty.com CRM; public docs, self-serve Bearer API key (free cloud tier or self-host), auto-generated REST and GraphQL endpoints from the workspace schema; community MCP servers exist (e.g. mhenry3164/twenty-crm-mcp-server).

apps.name: Stripe
apps.notes: Public REST docs; secret/restricted API keys with test keys available instantly on signup (OAuth2 used for Connect); Stripe ships an official MCP server at mcp.stripe.com.

apps.name: PitchBook
apps.notes: RESTful private-capital data API but a standalone paid contract via the Direct Data team is required and the actual API reference is client-gated (only marketing pages are public); PitchBook ships an MCP-based Premium Connector but it is limited to licensed enterprise users, and exact auth could not be fully verified behind the gate.

apps.name: DealCloud
apps.notes: Confirmed the Intapp DealCloud dealmaking CRM; docs are publicly viewable at api.docs.dealcloud.com and use OAuth2 client-credentials (client_id + apiKey), but access requires an enterprise DealCloud site sold via sales, and no MCP server was found.

apps.name: Paygent
apps.notes: Confirmed the Japanese Paygent Connect gateway (paygent.co.jp); a server-to-server HTTP module-type integration authenticated via client certificate plus merchant credentials/hash keys issued at contract, with the official spec available only behind the merchant portal (Japanese), so exact API shape could not be verified.

apps.name: iPayX
apps.notes: Confirmed the Internet Payment Exchange (IPayX) enterprise EBP/B2B payment provider; no public self-serve developer API or docs were found (enterprise/contract only); note the MCP server at ipayx.ai belongs to a different, unrelated FX-audit company.

apps.name: SendGrid
apps.notes: Twilio SendGrid v3 REST API with public docs and self-serve Bearer API keys (scoped, free tier available); community MCP servers exist (Garoth/sendgrid-mcp), plus a Twilio Alpha MCP.

apps.name: Meta Ads
apps.notes: Meta Marketing (Graph) API with public docs and OAuth2, but real/advanced access requires App Review plus business verification; Meta launched an official Ads MCP (mcp.facebook.com/ads, 2026 beta) and many community Meta Ads MCP servers also exist.

apps.name: Amazon SP-API
apps.notes: Amazon Selling Partner API with public docs and OAuth2 via Login with Amazon (AWS SigV4 was formerly required), but you must register as an SP-API developer and get app approval; Amazon publishes MCP dev tooling in the official amzn/selling-partner-api-samples repo and community SP-API MCPs exist.

apps.name: fanbasis
apps.notes: Confirmed the fanbasis.com creator-monetization/checkout platform; contrary to the gated expectation, public REST docs exist at apidocs.fan with a self-serve x-api-key from the dashboard, but you must first have a FanBasis merchant (Merchant-of-Record) account which likely involves onboarding/KYC; no MCP server found.

apps.name: Firecrawl
apps.notes: Public REST docs at docs.firecrawl.dev with a self-serve API key (free tier) or OAuth bearer token; Firecrawl ships an official MCP server (firecrawl/firecrawl-mcp-server).

apps.name: Waterfall.io
apps.notes: Confirmed the waterfall.io B2B mobile-identity/data-enrichment tool; docs are public at docs.waterfall.io (REST, x-api-key) but there is no self-serve signup - access is sales-gated behind Book-a-Call/dedicated onboarding; no MCP server found.

apps.name: OpenAI
apps.notes: Public REST docs and instant self-serve Bearer secret keys, but a payment method / prepaid credits must be added before the API will make successful calls (hence paid_gated); OpenAI has not shipped an official MCP server exposing its API, though community OpenAI MCP bridges exist.

apps.name: Devin
apps.notes: Confirmed the Cognition devin.ai AI software engineer; public REST API docs at docs.devin.ai with cog_ service API keys (personal access tokens in closed beta), but Devin has no free tier (paid plan required and API historically enterprise-oriented); Cognition ships an official Devin MCP server at mcp.devin.ai.

apps.name: Threads
apps.notes: Confirmed the Meta Threads API (developers.facebook.com/docs/threads); public docs, OAuth2 user access tokens only, but production beyond 25 test users requires Meta App Review; only community Threads MCP servers found (e.g. quinnjr/threads-mcp).
